channel.title: Nature Physics
channel.description: Nature Physics offers news and reviews alongside top-quality research papers in a monthly publication, covering the entire spectrum of physics. Physics addresses the properties and interactions of matter and energy, and plays a key role in the development of a broad range of technologies. To reflect this, Nature Physics covers all areas of pure and applied physics research. The journal focuses on core physics disciplines, but is also open to a broad range of topics whose central theme falls within the bounds of physics.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Dynamic magneto-chiral instability in photoexcited tellurium
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 09 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03145-8

Instabilities in chiral plasmas can amplify electromagnetic waves, raising the question of whether chiral solids behave similarly. Now a magneto-chiral instability is demonstrated in tellurium, observed as growing terahertz emission after photoexcitation.
item.dc:title: Dynamic magneto-chiral instability in photoexcited tellurium
item.dc:creator: Yijing Huang
Nick Abboud
Yinchuan Lv
Penghao Zhu
Azel Murzabekova
Changjun Lee
Emma A. Pappas
Dominic Petruzzi
Jason Y. Yan
Dipanjan Chaudhuri
Peter Abbamonte
Daniel P. Shoemaker
Rafael M. Fernandes
Jorge Noronha
Fahad Mahmood

item.title: A polyhedral structure controls programmable self-assembly
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 08 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03120-3

Programmable self-assembly can help construct complex nanostructures. Now a mathematical framework can identify if and how a particular structure can be assembled.
item.dc:title: A polyhedral structure controls programmable self-assembly
item.dc:creator: Maximilian C. Hübl
Thomas E. Videbæk
Daichi Hayakawa
W. Benjamin Rogers
Carl P. Goodrich

item.title: Optical control of orbital magnetism in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene
item.content:encoded:
Nature Physics, Published online: 06 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41567-025-03117-y

Strong correlations and topology have been seen in moiré graphene, but their optical control has not been shown yet. Now, the optical manipulation of orbital magnetism and anomalous Hall effects is demonstrated in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene.
item.dc:title: Optical control of orbital magnetism in magic-angle twisted bilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: Eylon Persky
Léonie Parisot
Minhao He
Jiaqi Cai
Takashi Taniguchi
Kenji Watanabe
Julian May-Mann
Xiaodong Xu
Aharon Kapitulnik
